+    published <- analysis_release_list(status = "published", conn = conn)
+    expect_identical(1L, length(published))
+  })
+})
+
+test_that("pre-insert re-read catches a source snapshot refreshed mid-build (fresh, not tautological)", {
+  with_release_build_db(function(conn) {
+    # The stateful loader returns snapshot_id 101 on the first functional read but
+    # snapshot_id 999 on the pre-insert re-read: if the re-read were tautological
+    # (re-checking the cached `loaded`) this would build; a FRESH read must catch it.
+    expect_error(
+      analysis_snapshot_release_build(
+        conn = conn, publish = TRUE,
+        loader = make_stateful_loader("functional_clusters"),
+        reproducibility_loader = present_repro_loader,
+        coherence_assert = pass_coherence
+      ),
+      class = "release_dependency_lineage_mismatch"
+    )
+    # nothing was persisted (the mismatch fired before insert):
+    expect_identical(0L, length(analysis_release_list(status = "published", conn = conn)))
+  })
+})
+
+test_that("build head + manifest carry the DB release provenance from the source snapshots (M1)", {
+  with_release_build_db(function(conn) {
+    result <- analysis_snapshot_release_build(
+      conn = conn, publish = TRUE,
+      loader = make_loader(), reproducibility_loader = present_repro_loader,
+      coherence_assert = pass_coherence
+    )
+    expect_equal(result$release$db_release_version, DB_RELEASE_VERSION)
+    expect_equal(result$release$db_release_commit, DB_RELEASE_COMMIT)
+
+    manifest_file <- analysis_release_get_file(result$release$release_id, "manifest.json", include_draft = TRUE, conn = conn)
+    manifest <- jsonlite::fromJSON(rawToChar(manifest_file$bytes), simplifyVector = FALSE)
+    expect_equal(manifest$source$db_release$version, DB_RELEASE_VERSION)
+    expect_equal(manifest$source$db_release$commit, DB_RELEASE_COMMIT)
+  })
+})
+
+test_that("insert duplicate-key race resolves to idempotent created=FALSE, no double insert (H3b)", {
+  with_release_build_db(function(conn) {
+    # The inserter simulates the concurrent WINNER: it stores the identical
+    # release, then this build's own insert loses the PK race (dup-key error).
+    dup_inserter <- function(head, members, files, conn) {
+      analysis_release_insert(head, members, files, conn)
+      stop("Duplicate entry 'asr_xxxx' for key 'PRIMARY'")
+    }
+    result <- analysis_snapshot_release_build(
+      conn = conn, publish = TRUE,
+      loader = make_loader(), reproducibility_loader = present_repro_loader,
+      coherence_assert = pass_coherence, inserter = dup_inserter
+    )
+    expect_false(result$created)
+    expect_match(result$release$release_id, "^asr_[0-9a-f]{16}$")
+    # exactly one row total -- the release was NOT double-inserted:
+    expect_identical(1L, length(analysis_release_list(status = NULL, conn = conn)))
+  })
+})
+
+test_that("build with publish = FALSE leaves a draft (not visible as published)", {
+  with_release_build_db(function(conn) {
+    result <- analysis_snapshot_release_build(
+      conn = conn, publish = FALSE,
+      loader = make_loader(), reproducibility_loader = present_repro_loader,
+      coherence_assert = pass_coherence
+    )
+    expect_true(result$created)
+    expect_equal(result$release$status, "draft")
+    expect_identical(0L, length(analysis_release_list(status = "published", conn = conn)))
+  })
+})
diff --git a/api/tests/testthat/test-unit-analysis-snapshot-coherence.R b/api/tests/testthat/test-unit-analysis-snapshot-coherence.R
index ef5cca74..ebf60579 100644
--- a/api/tests/testthat/test-unit-analysis-snapshot-coherence.R
+++ b/api/tests/testthat/test-unit-analysis-snapshot-coherence.R
@@ -160,3 +160,42 @@ test_that("join helper enforces member content coherence end to end (functional
   expect_error(analysis_snapshot_join_validated_clusters(mbad, v, kind = "functional"),
                "content", ignore.case = TRUE)
 })
+
+# --- #573 H4: reference member sets expressed in the STORED id space ---
+
+test_that("reference_members_store_space maps functional STRING_id -> hgnc_id via served identifiers", {
+  membership <- tibble::tibble(
+    cluster = c(1L, 2L),
+    identifiers = list(
+      tibble::tibble(STRING_id = c("9606.A", "9606.B"), hgnc_id = c("HGNC:1", "HGNC:2")),
+      tibble::tibble(STRING_id = c("9606.C"), hgnc_id = c("HGNC:3"))
+    )
+  )
+  ref <- list("1" = c("9606.A", "9606.B"), "2" = c("9606.C"))
+  out <- analysis_snapshot_reference_members_store_space(ref, membership, "functional")
+  expect_setequal(out[["1"]], c("HGNC:1", "HGNC:2"))
+  expect_setequal(out[["2"]], "HGNC:3")
+
+  # an UNMAPPED STRING_id is kept verbatim (fail-closed: cannot equal a stored hgnc_id)
+  ref2 <- list("1" = c("9606.A", "9606.UNKNOWN"))
+  out2 <- analysis_snapshot_reference_members_store_space(ref2, membership, "functional")
+  expect_setequal(out2[["1"]], c("HGNC:1", "9606.UNKNOWN"))
+})
+
+test_that("reference_members_store_space passes phenotype entity ids through unchanged", {
+  ref <- list("1" = c("10", "11"), "2" = c("12"))
+  out <- analysis_snapshot_reference_members_store_space(ref, NULL, "phenotype")
+  expect_setequal(out[["1"]], c("10", "11"))
+  expect_setequal(out[["2"]], "12")
+  expect_equal(length(analysis_snapshot_reference_members_store_space(list(), NULL, "phenotype")), 0L)
+})
+
+test_that("join attaches the store-space reference attestation for the builder to persist", {
+  members <- list("1" = c("9606.A", "9606.B"), "2" = c("9606.C", "9606.D"))
+  m <- mk_membership_with_members(members, id_col = "STRING_id")
+  v <- mk_val_with_members(members)
+  joined <- analysis_snapshot_join_validated_clusters(m, v, kind = "functional")
+  attest <- attr(joined, "reference_members_store_space")
+  expect_false(is.null(attest))
+  expect_setequal(names(attest), c("1", "2"))
+})
diff --git a/api/tests/testthat/test-unit-analysis-snapshot-release-service.R b/api/tests/testthat/test-unit-analysis-snapshot-release-service.R
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..91e8f8f7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/api/tests/testthat/test-unit-analysis-snapshot-release-service.R
@@ -0,0 +1,534 @@
+# tests/testthat/test-unit-analysis-snapshot-release-service.R
+#
+# Unit tests for the analysis-snapshot RELEASE service layer (#573 Slice A /
+# Task A5): api/services/analysis-snapshot-release-service.R.
+#
+# Pure unit tests, NO DATABASE. The A4 build orchestrator
+# (`analysis_snapshot_release_build`) and the A3 repository functions
+# (`analysis_release_list/get/get_file/get_bundle/publish/set_doi/
+# delete_draft`) are entirely STUBBED: this file never sources
+# `functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R` or
+# `functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R`, it only defines
+# minimal stand-ins for the names the service calls, then reassigns them
+# per-test via `with_release_mocks()`. This mirrors the established
+# `test-unit-metadata-vocabulary-service.R` mocking pattern: source
+# core/errors.R + the service with `source_api_file(local = FALSE)`, look up
+# `environment(<a service fn>)` to find where the service's free-variable
+# lookups resolve, then temporarily reassign bindings there.
+
+library(testthat)
+
+source_api_file("core/errors.R", local = FALSE)
+
+# Minimal stand-ins for the A3/A4 functions the service calls, so the service
+# file sources cleanly (its body only looks these names up at CALL time, but
+# defining them up front keeps `with_release_mocks()`'s get/assign symmetric
+# and self-documenting about the service's full dependency surface).
+analysis_snapshot_release_build <- function(...) stop("stub: analysis_snapshot_release_build not mocked")
+analysis_release_list <- function(...) stop("stub: analysis_release_list not mocked")
+analysis_release_get <- function(...) stop("stub: analysis_release_get not mocked")
+analysis_release_get_file <- function(...) stop("stub: analysis_release_get_file not mocked")
+analysis_release_get_bundle <- function(...) stop("stub: analysis_release_get_bundle not mocked")
+analysis_release_publish <- function(...) stop("stub: analysis_release_publish not mocked")
+analysis_release_set_doi <- function(...) stop("stub: analysis_release_set_doi not mocked")
+analysis_release_delete_draft <- function(...) stop("stub: analysis_release_delete_draft not mocked")
+# Identity stand-in for the PUBLIC projection: the REAL allowlist behaviour is
+# covered against the real function in the repository integration test; here the
+# service tests only verify svc_release_list/get ROUTE through it.
+analysis_release_public_head <- function(head) head
+
+source_api_file("services/analysis-snapshot-release-service.R", local = FALSE)
+
+# The environment the service functions were defined in (same top-level frame
+# the stand-ins above and core/errors.R were sourced into).
+release_svc_env <- environment(svc_release_build)
+
+#' Temporarily reassign a set of names in `release_svc_env`, restoring the
+#' previous bindings on exit (mirrors `with_repo_mocks()` in
+#' test-unit-metadata-vocabulary-service.R).
+with_release_mocks <- function(mocks, code) {
+  originals <- list()
+  for (name in names(mocks)) {
+    originals[[name]] <- get(name, envir = release_svc_env)
+    assign(name, mocks[[name]], envir = release_svc_env)
+  }
+  on.exit({
+    for (name in names(originals)) {
+      assign(name, originals[[name]], envir = release_svc_env)
+    }
+  }, add = TRUE)
+  force(code)
+}
+
+#' Build a classed condition matching A4's `c(<name>, "error", "condition")`
+#' shape (see functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R `.analysis_release_condition`).
+release_condition <- function(class_name, message) {
+  structure(
+    class = c(class_name, "error", "condition"),
+    list(message = message, call = NULL)
+  )
+}
+
+#' Minimal Plumber-response stand-in: a settable `$status` + a `setHeader()`
+#' that records headers (needed for the 503 + Retry-After lock path).
+release_fake_res <- function() {
+  res <- new.env()
+  res$status <- NULL
+  res$headers <- list()
+  res$setHeader <- function(name, value) {
+    res$headers[[name]] <- value
+    invisible(NULL)
+  }
+  res
+}
+
+# =============================================================================
+# svc_release_build
+# =============================================================================
+
+test_that("build success (created=TRUE) sets 201 and returns the head", {
+  head <- list(release_id = "asr_abc123", status = "published")
+  res <- release_fake_res()
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_snapshot_release_build = function(...) list(release = head, created = TRUE)),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_build(res)
+      expect_equal(res$status, 201L)
+      expect_identical(out, head)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("build idempotent (created=FALSE) sets 200 and returns the SAME head, no error", {
+  head <- list(release_id = "asr_dup456", status = "published")
+  res <- release_fake_res()
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_snapshot_release_build = function(...) list(release = head, created = FALSE)),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_build(res)
+      expect_equal(res$status, 200L)
+      expect_identical(out, head)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("build forwards layers/title/etc through to the orchestrator", {
+  captured <- NULL
+  res <- release_fake_res()
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_snapshot_release_build = function(...) {
+      captured <<- list(...)
+      list(release = list(release_id = "asr_x"), created = TRUE)
+    }),
+    {
+      svc_release_build(
+        res,
+        title = "My Title",
+        scope_statement = "scope",
+        license = "CC0-1.0",
+        publish = FALSE,
+        created_by = 7L,
+        conn = "conn-stub"
+      )
+    }
+  )
+  expect_equal(captured$title, "My Title")
+  expect_equal(captured$scope_statement, "scope")
+  expect_equal(captured$license, "CC0-1.0")
+  expect_false(captured$publish)
+  expect_equal(captured$created_by, 7L)
+  expect_equal(captured$conn, "conn-stub")
+  expect_false("layers" %in% names(captured)) # NULL layers -> not forwarded, orchestrator uses its own default
+})
+
+release_build_condition_cases <- list(
+  release_snapshot_not_available = "layer functional_clusters is not available for release: snapshot_missing",
+  release_source_incoherent = "layer functional_clusters failed the hard coherence re-check: boom",
+  release_reproducibility_missing = "layer phenotype_clusters has no reproducibility bundle; the release requires one",
+  release_source_version_mismatch = "shared source_data_version mismatch across layers: v1 vs v2",
+  release_dependency_lineage_mismatch = "layer phenotype_functional_correlations snapshot changed between read and insert"
+)
+
+test_that("build maps release_snapshot_not_available to a 400 carrying the reason", {
+  res <- release_fake_res()
+  msg <- release_build_condition_cases$release_snapshot_not_available
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_snapshot_release_build = function(...) stop(release_condition("release_snapshot_not_available", msg))),
+    {
+      err <- tryCatch(svc_release_build(res), error = function(e) e)
+      expect_s3_class(err, "error_400")
+      expect_match(conditionMessage(err), "functional_clusters", fixed = TRUE)
+      expect_match(conditionMessage(err), "snapshot_missing", fixed = TRUE)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("build maps release_source_incoherent to a 400 carrying the reason", {
+  res <- release_fake_res()
+  msg <- release_build_condition_cases$release_source_incoherent
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_snapshot_release_build = function(...) stop(release_condition("release_source_incoherent", msg))),
+    {
+      err <- tryCatch(svc_release_build(res), error = function(e) e)
+      expect_s3_class(err, "error_400")
+      expect_match(conditionMessage(err), "functional_clusters", fixed = TRUE)
+      expect_match(conditionMessage(err), "hard coherence", fixed = TRUE)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("build maps release_reproducibility_missing to a 400 carrying the reason", {
+  res <- release_fake_res()
+  msg <- release_build_condition_cases$release_reproducibility_missing
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_snapshot_release_build = function(...) stop(release_condition("release_reproducibility_missing", msg))),
+    {
+      err <- tryCatch(svc_release_build(res), error = function(e) e)
+      expect_s3_class(err, "error_400")
+      expect_match(conditionMessage(err), "phenotype_clusters", fixed = TRUE)
+      expect_match(conditionMessage(err), "reproducibility bundle", fixed = TRUE)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("build maps release_source_version_mismatch to a 400 carrying the reason", {
+  res <- release_fake_res()
+  msg <- release_build_condition_cases$release_source_version_mismatch
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_snapshot_release_build = function(...) stop(release_condition("release_source_version_mismatch", msg))),
+    {
+      err <- tryCatch(svc_release_build(res), error = function(e) e)
+      expect_s3_class(err, "error_400")
+      expect_match(conditionMessage(err), "v1 vs v2", fixed = TRUE)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("build maps release_dependency_lineage_mismatch to a 400 carrying the reason", {
+  res <- release_fake_res()
+  msg <- release_build_condition_cases$release_dependency_lineage_mismatch
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_snapshot_release_build = function(...) stop(release_condition("release_dependency_lineage_mismatch", msg))),
+    {
+      err <- tryCatch(svc_release_build(res), error = function(e) e)
+      expect_s3_class(err, "error_400")
+      expect_match(conditionMessage(err), "phenotype_functional_correlations", fixed = TRUE)
+      expect_match(conditionMessage(err), "changed between read and insert", fixed = TRUE)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("build maps release_lock_unavailable to a 503 + Retry-After (NOT a 400)", {
+  res <- release_fake_res()
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_snapshot_release_build = function(...) {
+      stop(release_condition("release_lock_unavailable", "sources are being refreshed; retry shortly"))
+    }),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_build(res)
+      expect_equal(res$status, 503L)
+      expect_equal(res$headers[["Retry-After"]], "5")
+      expect_equal(out$error, "release_lock_unavailable")
+      expect_match(out$message, "refreshed", fixed = TRUE)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("build lets a non-release_* error propagate unmapped (500 path)", {
+  res <- release_fake_res()
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_snapshot_release_build = function(...) stop("boom: unexpected DB error")),
+    {
+      err <- tryCatch(svc_release_build(res), error = function(e) e)
+      expect_false(inherits(err, "error_400"))
+      expect_false(inherits(err, "http_problem_error"))
+      expect_match(conditionMessage(err), "boom: unexpected DB error", fixed = TRUE)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+# =============================================================================
+# svc_release_publish
+# =============================================================================
+
+test_that("publish: unknown id -> 404", {
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(
+      analysis_release_publish = function(...) FALSE,
+      analysis_release_get = function(...) NULL
+    ),
+    {
+      expect_error(svc_release_publish("asr_missing"), class = "error_404")
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("publish: success returns the (now-published) head", {
+  head <- list(release_id = "asr_pub1", status = "published")
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(
+      analysis_release_publish = function(...) TRUE,
+      analysis_release_get = function(release_id, include_draft, conn = NULL) {
+        expect_true(include_draft)
+        head
+      }
+    ),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_publish("asr_pub1")
+      expect_identical(out, head)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+# =============================================================================
+# svc_release_set_doi
+# =============================================================================
+
+test_that("set_doi: unknown id -> 404", {
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(
+      analysis_release_set_doi = function(...) FALSE,
+      analysis_release_get = function(...) NULL
+    ),
+    {
+      expect_error(
+        svc_release_set_doi("asr_missing", list(version_doi = "10.5281/zenodo.1")),
+        class = "error_404"
+      )
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("set_doi: success returns the updated head and forwards doi_fields verbatim", {
+  head <- list(release_id = "asr_doi1", version_doi = "10.5281/zenodo.1")
+  captured <- NULL
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(
+      analysis_release_set_doi = function(release_id, doi_fields, conn = NULL) {
+        captured <<- doi_fields
+        TRUE
+      },
+      analysis_release_get = function(...) head
+    ),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_set_doi("asr_doi1", list(version_doi = "10.5281/zenodo.1"))
+      expect_identical(out, head)
+      expect_equal(captured$version_doi, "10.5281/zenodo.1")
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+# =============================================================================
+# svc_release_delete_draft
+# =============================================================================
+
+test_that("delete_draft: unknown id -> 404", {
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_get = function(...) NULL),
+    {
+      expect_error(svc_release_delete_draft("asr_missing"), class = "error_404")
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("delete_draft: published release -> 400 with the exact reason message", {
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_get = function(...) list(release_id = "asr_pub", status = "published")),
+    {
+      err <- tryCatch(svc_release_delete_draft("asr_pub"), error = function(e) e)
+      expect_s3_class(err, "error_400")
+      expect_equal(
+        conditionMessage(err),
+        "Cannot delete a published release; only drafts are deletable"
+      )
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("delete_draft: draft release deletes and returns deleted=TRUE", {
+  delete_called_with <- NULL
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(
+      analysis_release_get = function(...) list(release_id = "asr_draft1", status = "draft"),
+      analysis_release_delete_draft = function(release_id, conn = NULL) {
+        delete_called_with <<- release_id
+        TRUE
+      }
+    ),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_delete_draft("asr_draft1")
+      expect_equal(out, list(deleted = TRUE, release_id = "asr_draft1"))
+      expect_equal(delete_called_with, "asr_draft1")
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+# =============================================================================
+# svc_release_list (public)
+# =============================================================================
+
+test_that("list: reads only status='published' from the repository and projects each head", {
+  captured <- NULL
+  rows <- list(list(release_id = "asr_1"), list(release_id = "asr_2"))
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_list = function(status, limit, offset, conn = NULL) {
+      captured <<- list(status = status, limit = limit, offset = offset)
+      rows
+    }),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_list(limit = 10, offset = 5)
+      expect_identical(out, rows) # identity projection stub -> verbatim
+      expect_equal(captured$status, "published")
+      expect_equal(captured$limit, 10L)
+      expect_equal(captured$offset, 5L)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("list: clamps limit to [1,100] and offset to >=0 (L1)", {
+  captured <- NULL
+  capture_loader <- function(status, limit, offset, conn = NULL) {
+    captured <<- list(limit = limit, offset = offset)
+    list()
+  }
+  cases <- list(
+    list(in_limit = -1, in_offset = -5, out_limit = 1L, out_offset = 0L),
+    list(in_limit = 1e6, in_offset = 10, out_limit = 100L, out_offset = 10L),
+    list(in_limit = "abc", in_offset = "xyz", out_limit = 50L, out_offset = 0L)
+  )
+  with_release_mocks(list(analysis_release_list = capture_loader), {
+    for (case in cases) {
+      svc_release_list(limit = case$in_limit, offset = case$in_offset)
+      expect_equal(captured$limit, case$out_limit)
+      expect_equal(captured$offset, case$out_offset)
+    }
+  })
+})
+
+test_that("list + get route heads through analysis_release_public_head (H1)", {
+  marker <- list(release_id = "asr_projected", projected = TRUE)
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(
+      analysis_release_list = function(...) list(list(release_id = "asr_raw", created_by_user_id = 9L)),
+      analysis_release_get = function(...) list(release_id = "asr_raw", created_by_user_id = 9L),
+      analysis_release_public_head = function(head) marker
+    ),
+    {
+      expect_identical(svc_release_list()[[1]], marker)
+      expect_identical(svc_release_get("asr_raw"), marker)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+# =============================================================================
+# svc_release_get (public)
+# =============================================================================
+
+test_that("get: draft or unknown (stub returns NULL) -> 404, and include_draft is FALSE", {
+  captured_include_draft <- NULL
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_get = function(release_id, include_draft, conn = NULL) {
+      captured_include_draft <<- include_draft
+      NULL
+    }),
+    {
+      expect_error(svc_release_get("asr_draft_or_missing"), class = "error_404")
+    }
+  )
+  expect_false(captured_include_draft)
+})
+
+test_that("get: success returns the head", {
+  head <- list(release_id = "asr_pub2", status = "published")
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_get = function(...) head),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_get("asr_pub2")
+      expect_identical(out, head)
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+# =============================================================================
+# svc_release_manifest (public)
+# =============================================================================
+
+test_that("manifest: unknown -> 404", {
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_get_file = function(...) NULL),
+    {
+      expect_error(svc_release_manifest("asr_missing"), class = "error_404")
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("manifest: returns {bytes, media_type='application/json', content_sha256} and asks for manifest.json only", {
+  captured_path <- NULL
+  bytes <- charToRaw('{"release_id":"asr_1"}')
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_get_file = function(release_id, file_path, include_draft, conn = NULL) {
+      captured_path <<- file_path
+      expect_false(include_draft)
+      list(bytes = bytes, media_type = "application/json", content_sha256 = "abc123")
+    }),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_manifest("asr_1")
+      expect_identical(out, list(bytes = bytes, media_type = "application/json", content_sha256 = "abc123"))
+    }
+  )
+  expect_equal(captured_path, "manifest.json")
+})
+
+# =============================================================================
+# svc_release_file (public)
+# =============================================================================
+
+test_that("file: unknown path -> 404", {
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_get_file = function(...) NULL),
+    {
+      expect_error(svc_release_file("asr_1", "does/not/exist.json"), class = "error_404")
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("file: returns {bytes, media_type, content_sha256} for a known path", {
+  bytes <- charToRaw("# README")
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_get_file = function(release_id, file_path, include_draft, conn = NULL) {
+      expect_false(include_draft)
+      list(bytes = bytes, media_type = "text/markdown", content_sha256 = "def456")
+    }),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_file("asr_1", "README.md")
+      expect_identical(out, list(bytes = bytes, media_type = "text/markdown", content_sha256 = "def456"))
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+# =============================================================================
+# svc_release_bundle (public)
+# =============================================================================
+
+test_that("bundle: unknown -> 404", {
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_get_bundle = function(...) NULL),
+    {
+      expect_error(svc_release_bundle("asr_missing"), class = "error_404")
+    }
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("bundle: returns {bytes, sha256, filename}", {
+  bytes <- as.raw(c(1, 2, 3))
+  with_release_mocks(
+    list(analysis_release_get_bundle = function(release_id, include_draft, conn = NULL) {
+      expect_false(include_draft)
+      list(bytes = bytes, sha256 = "ghi789", filename = "asr_1.tar.gz")
+    }),
+    {
+      out <- svc_release_bundle("asr_1")
+      expect_identical(out, list(bytes = bytes, sha256 = "ghi789", filename = "asr_1.tar.gz"))
+    }
+  )
+})

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-1-# Pure, DB-free helpers for immutable public analysis-snapshot RELEASES
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-2-# (#573 Slice A / Task A2): the layer registry, content-address identity
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R:3:# (content_digest / release_id), the manifest.json / checksums.sha256
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-4-# builders, and the deterministic tar.gz archive writer.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-5-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-6-# These functions define release IDENTITY and file contracts consumed by
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-7-# later tasks (repository persistence, build orchestrator). They must stay
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-8-# pure: no DB access, no network, no side effects beyond a scratch tempdir
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-9-# used internally by `analysis_release_build_tar_gz()`.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-10-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-11-# Reuses the EXISTING canonical JSON serializer from
--
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-50-#' Resolve a caller-supplied `layers` request to authoritative REGISTRY entries.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-51-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-52-#' `layers` in a build request is a SELECTION, never a policy redefinition: each
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-53-#' requested entry is read ONLY for its `analysis_type` (accepting either a bare
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-54-#' string or a `{analysis_type, ...}` object), matched against the authoritative
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-55-#' `analysis_snapshot_release_layers()` registry, and the REGISTRY entry is
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-56-#' returned — so the caller can never override `params`, `files_prefix`, or the
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-57-#' gate-controlling `has_reproducibility` (which would let an Admin skip the hard
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R:58:#' coherence / reproducibility gates, or path-traverse via `files_prefix`).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-59-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-60-#' NULL/absent `requested` -> the full registry unchanged. An unknown or
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-61-#' duplicated `analysis_type` -> 400 (`stop_for_bad_request`).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-62-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-63-#' @param requested NULL, or a list of selectors (strings or `{analysis_type}`).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-64-#' @return list of registry layer entries (a subset of the registry, in request
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-65-#'   order).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-66-analysis_snapshot_release_resolve_layers <- function(requested = NULL) {
--
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-142-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-143-#' Deliberately excludes `created_at`, `title`, and any DOI — release
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-144-#' identity is pure scientific content (each layer's input/payload/
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-145-#' reproducibility hashes and dependencies, plus the source data version and
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-146-#' manifest schema version), never presentation metadata. `layer_entries` is
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-147-#' sorted by `analysis_type` before hashing so caller-supplied ordering never
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-148-#' changes the digest.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-149-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R:150:#' @param layer_entries list of list(analysis_type, input_hash, payload_hash,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-151-#'   reproducibility_hash, dependencies).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-152-#' @param source_data_version chr.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-153-#' @param manifest_schema_version chr.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-154-#' @return chr, a 64-character lowercase hex sha256 digest.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R:155:analysis_release_content_digest <- function(layer_entries, source_data_version, manifest_schema_version) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-156-  analysis_types <- vapply(layer_entries, function(entry) entry$analysis_type, character(1))
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-157-  # method = "radix" is locale-invariant: the content identity must not depend on
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-158-  # the builder's LC_COLLATE (de-risks cross-host #574 reproducibility).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-159-  sorted_entries <- layer_entries[order(analysis_types, method = "radix")]
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-160-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-161-  identity_layers <- lapply(sorted_entries, function(entry) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R:162:    entry[c("analysis_type", "input_hash", "payload_hash", "reproducibility_hash", "dependencies")]
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-163-  })
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-164-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-165-  identity_obj <- list(
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-166-    manifest_schema_version = manifest_schema_version,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-167-    source_data_version = source_data_version,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-168-    layers = identity_layers
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-169-  )
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-170-
--
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-172-}
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-173-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-174-#' Short, readable release handle derived from the content digest.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-175-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-176-#' The first 16 hex characters (64 bits) of the content digest, prefixed
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-177-#' `asr_`. This is a human/URL-facing handle only; the full content digest is
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-178-#' the authoritative identity value and is stored separately by later tasks.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-179-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R:180:#' @param content_digest chr, as returned by `analysis_release_content_digest()`.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-181-#' @return chr, matching `^asr_[0-9a-f]{16}$` for a well-formed digest.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R:182:analysis_release_id <- function(content_digest) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R:183:  paste0("asr_", substr(content_digest, 1, 16))
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-184-}
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-185-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-186-#' Build the release `manifest.json` R list.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-187-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-188-#' `fields$files` is the caller-computed flat file list (one
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-189-#' `list(path, sha256, bytes)` entry per archive member). Neither
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-190-#' `manifest.json` nor `checksums.sha256` can describe their own checksum, so
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-191-#' both are excluded from the `files[]` array in the built manifest.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-192-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-193-#' @param fields list with elements `release_id`, `release_version`, `title`,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-194-#'   `created_at`, `license`, `scope_statement`, `generator`, `source`,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R:195:#'   `layers`, `files`, `content_digest`.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-196-#' @return list, the manifest ready for `analysis_snapshot_canonical_json()`.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-197-analysis_release_build_manifest <- function(fields) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-198-  self_describing_paths <- c("manifest.json", "checksums.sha256")
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-199-  files <- Filter(function(f) !(f$path %in% self_describing_paths), fields$files)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-200-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-201-  list(
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-202-    release_id = fields$release_id,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-203-    release_version = fields$release_version,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-204-    title = fields$title,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-205-    created_at = fields$created_at,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-206-    license = fields$license,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-207-    scope_statement = fields$scope_statement,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-208-    generator = fields$generator,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-209-    source = fields$source,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-210-    layers = fields$layers,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-211-    files = files,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R:212:    content_digest = fields$content_digest
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-213-  )
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-214-}
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-215-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-216-#' Build the `checksums.sha256` file content.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-217-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R-218-#' One `"<sha256>  <path>\n"` line per file (the classic `sha256sum` format),

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R-77-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R-78-# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R-79-# Write
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R-80-# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R-81-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R-82-#' Insert a release head + its members + its files in ONE transaction.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R-83-#'
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R-84-#' `release_head` is a named list with (at least) `release_id`,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R:85:#' `manifest_schema_version`, `content_digest`, `manifest_sha256`,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R-86-#' `bundle_sha256`, `bundle_gzip` (raw), plus optional `release_version`,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R:87:#' `title`, `source_data_version`, `db_release_version`, `db_release_commit`,

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-1-# functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-2-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-3-# Build orchestrator for immutable, content-addressed public analysis-snapshot
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-4-# RELEASES (#573 Slice A / Task A4). This is the correctness-critical layer: it
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:5:# LOADS the active public snapshots, GATES them (available + hard coherence +
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-6-# reproducibility presence + shared source-data version + dependency lineage +
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-7-# TOCTOU), MATERIALIZES the release files, computes the content-addressed
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-8-# identity, and PERSISTS via the A3 repository.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-9-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-10-# Reuses (sourced by callers before this file / registered in load_modules):
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-11-#   - analysis-snapshot-presets.R          : canonical JSON + parameter hashing
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:12:#   - analysis-snapshot-coherence.R        : analysis_snapshot_assert_partition_coherent
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-13-#   - analysis-snapshot-dependencies.R     : analysis_snapshot_manifest_dependencies
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-14-#   - analysis-reproducibility.R           : analysis_reproducibility_decode_raw
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-15-#   - analysis-snapshot-release-manifest.R : identity + manifest/checksums/tar helpers (A2)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-16-#   - analysis-snapshot-release-repository.R: analysis_release_insert/get/exists/... (A3)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-17-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-18-# DEPENDENCY-INJECTION SEAMS. The three collaborators below are injectable so the
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-19-# gates are deterministically unit-testable WITHOUT seeding the complex snapshot
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-20-# tables. Their call-time defaults are the real functions; the body always calls
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-21-# the seam, never the real function directly:
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-22-#   - loader(analysis_type, parameter_hash, conn)  -> loaded snapshot
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-23-#   - reproducibility_loader(snapshot_id, conn)    -> reproducibility row (or NULL)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:24:#   - coherence_assert(snapshot, kind)             -> invisibly / throws
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-25-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-26-# ERROR CONTRACT. The five classed conditions below are `c(<name>,"error",
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-27-# "condition")`; the A5 service maps them to HTTP 400 (the contract has no 409):
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:28:#   release_snapshot_not_available, release_source_incoherent,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:29:#   release_reproducibility_missing, release_source_version_mismatch,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-30-#   release_dependency_lineage_mismatch.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-31-# A DUPLICATE build is NOT an error: it returns the existing head with
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-32-# created = FALSE (service -> 200).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-33-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-34-if (!exists("%||%", mode = "function")) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-35-  `%||%` <- function(x, y) if (is.null(x)) y else x
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-36-}
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-37-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:38:# Materialization / gate / coherence helpers live in the sibling file (kept
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-39-# separate so both stay under the 600-line ceiling). Registered together in
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-40-# bootstrap/load_modules.R -- Task A8 -- and sourced together by the integration test.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-41-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-42-# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-43-# Advisory locks (TOCTOU): serialize a build against a concurrent axis refresh.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-44-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:45:# The build acquires the SAME per-preset advisory lock the axis refresh holds —
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-46-# `analysis_snapshot_lock_name(analysis_type, parameter_hash)` — so a mid-flight
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-47-# refresh of a source preset blocks the read (MySQL 8 lets one session hold many
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-48-# named GET_LOCKs). Best-effort: engaged only on a real DBIConnection (a
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-49-# pooled/NULL conn cannot hold a session-scoped GET_LOCK meaningfully), and a
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:50:# lock-acquire timeout does NOT abort — the fresh pre-insert re-read below is the
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-51-# invariant that always catches a snapshot that changed under us.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-52-# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-53-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-54-#' Per-preset lock name — identical to the axis-refresh lock so they collide.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-55-#' @noRd
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-56-.analysis_release_preset_lock_name <- function(analysis_type, parameter_hash) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-57-  if (exists("analysis_snapshot_lock_name", mode = "function")) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-58-    return(analysis_snapshot_lock_name(analysis_type, parameter_hash))
--
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-112-    } else {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-113-      for (held in acquired) .analysis_release_release_named_lock(conn, held)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-114-      return(list(ok = FALSE, acquired = character(0), skipped = FALSE))
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-115-    }
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-116-  }
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-117-  list(ok = TRUE, acquired = acquired, skipped = FALSE)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-118-}
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-119-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:120:#' Fresh pre-insert re-read: re-load each layer via the loader seam (NOT the
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:121:#' cached step-1 `loaded`) and confirm each layer's {snapshot_id, payload_hash}
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-122-#' and the correlation dependencies still equal the pinned lineage. Throws a
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-123-#' classed gate error if a source snapshot changed between the first read and the
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-124-#' insert (the real TOCTOU catch).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-125-#' @noRd
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-126-.analysis_release_verify_lineage_unchanged <- function(layer_specs, loaded, loader, conn) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-127-  for (spec in layer_specs) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-128-    at <- spec$analysis_type
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-129-    entry <- loaded[[at]]
--
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-132-    if (!identical(status_code, "available")) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-133-      stop(.analysis_release_condition(
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-134-        "release_snapshot_not_available",
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-135-        sprintf("layer %s became unavailable before insert: %s", at, status_code),
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-136-        analysis_type = at, status_code = status_code
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-137-      ))
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-138-    }
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-139-    fresh_id <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(.analysis_release_manifest_scalar(fresh$manifest, "snapshot_id")))
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:140:    fresh_hash <- as.character(.analysis_release_manifest_scalar(fresh$manifest, "payload_hash", NA_character_))
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-141-    if (!identical(fresh_id, suppressWarnings(as.integer(entry$snapshot_id))) ||
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:142:      !identical(fresh_hash, entry$payload_hash)) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-143-      stop(.analysis_release_condition(
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-144-        "release_dependency_lineage_mismatch",
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-145-        sprintf(
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-146-          "layer %s snapshot changed between read and insert (was snapshot_id %s, now %s)",
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-147-          at, as.character(entry$snapshot_id), as.character(fresh_id)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-148-        ),
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-149-        analysis_type = at
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-150-      ))

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-176-#' @param title,scope_statement,license Presentation metadata (excluded from the
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-177-#'   content digest / release identity).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-178-#' @param publish If TRUE the inserted draft is flipped to `published`.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-179-#' @param created_by Optional user id recorded on the head row.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-180-#' @param conn A real DBIConnection (required for persistence; A5 checks one out).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-181-#' @param layers Optional SELECTION of layers to include (NULL = full registry);
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-182-#'   resolved to authoritative registry entries by
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-183-#'   `analysis_snapshot_release_resolve_layers()` (caller cannot override policy).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R:184:#' @param loader,reproducibility_loader,coherence_assert,lock_acquire,inserter
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-185-#'   Injectable seams (see file header); call-time defaults are the real
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-186-#'   functions. `lock_acquire` returns `list(ok, acquired, skipped)`; `inserter`
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-187-#'   persists the head/members/files.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-188-#' @return `list(release = <head>, created = TRUE|FALSE)`.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-189-#' @export
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-190-analysis_snapshot_release_build <- function(layers = NULL,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-191-                                            title = NULL,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-192-                                            scope_statement = NULL,

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-200-                                            lock_acquire = .analysis_release_acquire_preset_locks,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-201-                                            inserter = analysis_release_insert) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-202-  # B1: a caller `layers` request is a SELECTION, never a policy redefinition —
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-203-  # resolve each selector to the AUTHORITATIVE registry entry (params /
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-204-  # files_prefix / has_reproducibility come from the registry, never the caller).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-205-  layers <- analysis_snapshot_release_resolve_layers(layers)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-206-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-207-  # Resolve (analysis_type, parameter_hash) per layer once (pure; validates params).

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-210-    list(

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-219-  # refresh of a source preset serializes against this read. On a REAL
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-220-  # DBIConnection a failed acquisition MUST NOT proceed unlocked (H3): it raises
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release.R-221-  # `release_lock_unavailable`, which the service maps to 503 + Retry-After.

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-2-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-3-# Pure, DB-light helpers for the immutable analysis-snapshot RELEASE build
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-4-# orchestrator (#573 Slice A / Task A4). Split out of
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-5-# `analysis-snapshot-release.R` to keep both files under the 600-line ceiling.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-6-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-7-# Contains: the classed-condition constructor, the manifest/loader extraction
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:8:# helpers, the best-effort HARD coherence-re-check default (`coherence_assert`
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-9-# seam default), the cross-layer lineage gates, and the per-layer file
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-10-# materialization (payload / reproducibility / README). No DB access, no network.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-11-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-12-# Sourced together with `analysis-snapshot-release.R` (both registered in
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-13-# `bootstrap/load_modules.R` -- Task A8 -- and both sourced by the direct-source
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-14-# integration test).

--

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:60:#' values that DISAGREE across layers raise `release_source_version_mismatch`
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-61-#' (mirroring the source_data_version gate — the service maps it to 400); empty
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-62-#' NA values are ignored (not a conflict). When `strict = FALSE`, a disagreement
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-63-#' silently takes the first non-empty value.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-64-#' @noRd

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-68-    function(e) as.character(.analysis_release_manifest_scalar(e$manifest, field, NA_character_)),
--
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-70-  )

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-122-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-123-# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:124:# Default coherence seam (best-effort HARD re-check over the STORED snapshot).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-125-#
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:126:# Two of the three #514 coherence components ARE reconstructable from the stored
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:127:# public snapshot and are re-checked here (HARD, require_coherence = TRUE,
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-128-# ignoring the ANALYSIS_SNAPSHOT_REQUIRE_COHERENCE downgrade):
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-129-#   1. Cluster-set integrity: every visible cluster (by cluster_kind) must appear
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-130-#      in the membership AND carry a non-NA stability score (jaccard_mean) in its
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-131-#      metadata_json — directly catching the #514 symptom ("real clusters with n/a
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-132-#      stability") in stored form.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-133-#   2. Channel match (functional axis): the served membership channel
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-134-#      (`membership_weight_channel`) and the validation channel (`weight_channel`)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:135:#      are both persisted in the manifest `validation_json`; when both are present
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-136-#      they must agree, else the served membership was clustered on a different
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-137-#      STRING channel than the validation scored (the #514 text-mining-vs-exp+db
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-138-#      case that slips through when cluster-id labels coincide).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-139-# The THIRD component — full member-set equality — is genuinely NOT
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:140:# reconstructable: the validator's `reference_members` is a sibling of `partition`
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-141-# and is never persisted. That check is left to the build-time gate; the dev-stack
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-142-# e2e exercises the true membership-vs-validation recompute path.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-143-# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-144-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:145:#' Parse the manifest `validation_json` column to a plain list (or empty list).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-146-#' @noRd
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:147:.analysis_release_parse_validation_json <- function(manifest) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:148:  raw <- suppressWarnings(as.character(.analysis_release_manifest_scalar(manifest, "validation_json", NA_character_)))
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-149-  if (length(raw) == 0L || is.na(raw[[1]]) || !nzchar(raw[[1]])) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-150-    return(list())
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-151-  }
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-152-  parsed <- tryCatch(jsonlite::fromJSON(raw[[1]], simplifyVector = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-153-  if (is.null(parsed) || !is.list(parsed)) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-154-    return(list())
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-155-  }
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-156-  parsed
--
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-165-  parsed <- tryCatch(jsonlite::fromJSON(txt[[1]], simplifyVector = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-166-  if (is.null(parsed)) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-167-    return(FALSE)
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-168-  }
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-169-  score <- parsed$jaccard_mean %||% parsed$stability %||% parsed$stability_score
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-170-  !is.null(score) && length(score) >= 1L && !is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(score[[1]])))
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-171-}
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-172-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:173:#' Hard coherence re-check over a loaded cluster snapshot. Default `coherence_assert`.
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-174-#' @export
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:175:analysis_snapshot_release_assert_coherent <- function(snapshot, kind) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-176-  clusters <- .analysis_release_rows(snapshot$clusters)

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-178-  if ("cluster_kind" %in% names(clusters)) {

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-180-  }
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-181-  if ("cluster_kind" %in% names(members)) {

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-183-  }

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-195-    )
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-196-    valid_ids <- as.character(clusters$cluster_id[has_stability])

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-199-

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-201-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:202:  # Channel match (functional axis only): both channels live in validation_json;
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-203-  # when both are present they must agree. Absent/older snapshots skip this
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:204:  # comparison (assert_partition_coherent only fires channel_mismatch when BOTH

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-208-  if (identical(kind, "functional")) {

api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-211-  }
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-212-
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-213-  # H4: MEMBER-SET proof. Reconstruct the served membership member sets from the
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-214-  # stored cluster_members (grouped by cluster_id, in the stored id space:
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-215-  # hgnc_id for functional / entity_id for phenotype) and compare them against the
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:216:  # persisted validator reference sets (validation_json$reference_members, same
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-217-  # stored space). When the attestation is ABSENT (legacy pre-#573 snapshot),
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-218-  # GRACEFULLY DEGRADE to the channel + stability check and WARN — never hard
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-219-  # reject a legacy snapshot (that would block every release until a full rebuild).
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-220-  member_col <- if (identical(kind, "functional")) "hgnc_id" else "entity_id"
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-221-  membership_members <- NULL
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-222-  if (all(c("cluster_id", member_col) %in% names(members)) && nrow(members) > 0L) {
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-223-    grouped <- split(as.character(members[[member_col]]), as.character(members$cluster_id))
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-224-    membership_members <- lapply(grouped, function(v) unique(v[!is.na(v) & nzchar(v)]))
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R-225-  }
api/functions/analysis-snapshot-release-materialize.R:226:  validation_members <- validation$reference_members

api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R-6-# ARCHITECTURE: the orchestrator has three dependency-injection seams so the
api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R-7-# correctness-critical GATES are deterministically unit-testable WITHOUT seeding
api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R-8-# the (very complex) analysis_snapshot_* source tables:
api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R-9-#   - loader(analysis_type, parameter_hash, conn)  (default analysis_snapshot_get_public)
api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R-10-#   - reproducibility_loader(snapshot_id, conn)    (default analysis_snapshot_get_reproducibility)
api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R:11:#   - coherence_assert(snapshot, kind)             (default analysis_snapshot_release_assert_coherent)
api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R-12-# Every gate branch is driven by injecting fakes. PERSISTENCE (analysis_release_*)
api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R-13-# runs against the REAL release tables (ensure_test_release_schema), so
api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R-14-# idempotency/persistence is genuinely exercised even while the snapshot SOURCE is
api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R-15-# faked. The real seam DEFAULTS are exercised by the post-slice dev-stack e2e.
api/tests/testthat/test-integration-analysis-snapshot-release-build.R-16-#
--
